ledger_meta.name: TownWatch State Expansion Ledger
ledger_meta.purpose: Phase status per state for the three-tier expansion model. Drives the queue; demand signals reorder it. See STATE_PLAYBOOK.md for phase definitions, exit criteria, and the GA exemplar.
notes: Quirks marked 'prior' are general-knowledge expectations, NOT verified — Phase B/C must confirm or refute them before any reliance.
Queue policy: adjacency-first from the GA footprint; demand signals from the adopt funnel reorder; money never gates the map.
Territories (PR, GU, VI, AS, MP) deferred pending mission scope — add rows when in scope.

quirks.note: Public-records requests restricted to state citizens (enforceable per McBurney v. Young) — records automation needs the citizen-proxy lane; confirm statute in Phase A.
Boroughs + the Unorganized Borough; several unified home-rule municipalities (Juneau, Anchorage); REAAs for schools.
Has well-known city-county consolidations to handle in Phase C bundle rules.
Separate elementary/secondary districts — seed job must ingest Census elsd/scsd files, not unsd only (BACKLOG).
Public-records requests restricted to state citizens (enforceable per McBurney v. Young) — records automation needs the citizen-proxy lane; confirm statute in Phase A.
Separate elementary/secondary districts — seed job must ingest Census elsd/scsd files, not unsd only (BACKLOG).
Has well-known city-county consolidations to handle in Phase C bundle rules.
Has well-known city-county consolidations to handle in Phase C bundle rules.
Municipal government is largely the Census COUSUB (town/township) layer; the place-based universe model misses it — Tier 0 blocked on the COUSUB layer (BACKLOG).
Supervisory unions / regional districts — bundle rule needs a multi-member concept.
County governments abolished or vestigial — county layer is geography, not government, here.
Public-records requests restricted to state citizens (enforceable per McBurney v. Young) — records automation needs the citizen-proxy lane; confirm statute in Phase A.

DC.name: District of Columbia

quirks.note: One statewide/district-wide school system.

DC.notes: Single-row special case: one general-purpose government + one school district (DCPS) + charter sector; federal overlay (Home Rule). Tier 1 is small; FOIA is DC's own act.

GA.name: Georgia

quirks.note: 8 consolidated city-county governments (DCA-verified): Athens-Clarke, Augusta-Richmond, Columbus-Muscogee, Macon-Bibb, Cusseta-Chattahoochee, Georgetown-Quitman, Echols, Webster. All bundle to their county in jurisdiction_directory.
GA dissolves dead municipalities by statute (Ranger HB 773 2023; Sunny Side HB 542 eff. 2024-01-01) — seed job cleanup handles.
Fort Stewart School District (1300003) is DoDEA federal — excluded from the directory.

GA.notes: Exemplar state. Universe: 159 counties + 536 municipalities + 180 school systems = 875 (directory: 538/159/180, 188 bundled). Recon: 66 registry entries (21 counties, 11 cities, 34 school districts incl. consolidated Augusta). Onboarded: grovetown, columbia-county, columbia-county-school-district + documented-absence placeholders glascock-county-consolidated-schools, taliaferro-county-school-district. Platform census: Simbli 27/33 school districts; CivicClerk/CivicPlus dominant for counties/cities. Pipeline-delta items in townwatch-web/BACKLOG.md ('Pipeline hardening', 2026-06-10).

SC.name: South Carolina
SC.priority_reason: Adjacency-first: the Augusta metro spills into Aiken and Edgefield counties (North Augusta) — the existing CSRA footprint already wants it.

quirks.note: SC's open-records AND open-meetings law are ONE statute (Title 30, Ch. 4 FOIA), unlike GA's two acts. Verified scstatehouse.gov 2026-06-13.

SC's sharpest, most observable duty is PRE-meeting: § 30-4-80 agenda must be posted on the body's public website (if any) >=24h before the meeting. Inverse of GA (post-meeting summary). agenda_missing is SC's primary Tier-2 recon dimension.

§ 30-4-90 minutes due only 'within a reasonable time'; NO numeric deadline and NO post-meeting summary tier. GA's hard-clock minutes-overdue observer does NOT transfer to SC — judge against the body's own cadence, never an asserted statutory deadline.

§ 30-4-30 grants records access to 'a person' (no residency limit) -> direct-send lane. BUT enforcement standing (§ 30-4-100) is 'a citizen of the State' -> ORR-to-suit escalation needs a SC-citizen requester.

County & municipal candidates file campaign-disclosure reports electronically & centrally with the SC State Ethics Commission (§ 8-13-100(2), § 8-13-365), public at ethicsfiling.sc.gov — NOT a local clerk. Post-Act-184 (2018) regime.

School districts are sub-county in places (multiple districts per county); some districts fiscally dependent (county council sets the levy). Confirm structure against CCD in Phase B/C — affects bundle map + who the levying authority is.

Confirmed in Dorchester County (2026-06-13): a single county can hold multiple independent unified school districts. Dorchester has TWO active — Dorchester School District Two (Summerville) and Dorchester School District Four (St. George); former Districts 1 & 3 were consolidated into District 4 by SC Act, 1985-86. Bundle rule: county fund covers county + BOTH districts riding it.

Dorchester County runs the legacy Granicus stack (dorchester.granicus.com/AgendaViewer.php + /services/minutes/reports/<uuid>/attachment + granicus S3 packets), bucketed as granicus_legistar in the recon vocab. Not Legistar/IQM2. Existing Granicus client work should cover it.

SC bills 3154 (2023-24) and 3470 (2025-26) study merging Dorchester DD2 + DD4; barred without approval by registered electors of each district. Track for jurisdiction-structure changes before/after onboarding.

Dorchester County's official .gov (Granicus govAccess CMS) is bot-walled (HTTP 403) — needs the browser fetch tier for nav/listing pages. Mitigations confirmed: Granicus agenda/minutes platform, NextRequest FOIA portal, and granicus S3 document attachments all sit OUTSIDE the wall.

demand_signals.signal: Operator-directed onboarding prep for Dorchester County (Charleston/Lowcountry metro). Tier-2 recon run AHEAD of SC's Tier-1 dossier as a demand pull; reorders away from the original Augusta/CSRA adjacency rationale for SC's queue slot.

SC.notes: Phase A statute inventory: FOIA CORE COMPLETE & verified 2026-06-13 (primary sources scstatehouse.gov Title 30 Ch.4 + Title 8 Ch.13; adversarial 3-of-3 all upheld). 6 finding categories derived. REMAINING Phase-A: finance-transparency layer (budget/millage/audit, SC Titles 4/5/6/12/59) deferred as TODO in the SC._sc_finance_layer_todo block — no finance ingestion exists so nothing activated by deferring; do NOT port GA OCGA finance cites. EXECUTION NOTE: Tier-2 recon went DEMAND-FIRST (Dorchester County / Lowcountry — see demand_signals + artifacts.recon_registry) AHEAD of the Tier-1 dossier; priority_reason (Augusta/CSRA adjacency) remains the standing structural rationale and the eventual dossier batch still starts there. Phase B universe verification (statewide CCD reconciliation of sub-county district structure + the fiscal-dependency clause, neither yet verified) still pending before broad Tier-2.

quirks.note: Townships exist with limited functions; decide in Phase C whether they are audit-relevant governments here.
